channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: Opinion: Opinion | Why Was A Russian Doomsday Plane In Iran Recently? Inside The 'Tu-214PU'
item.description: A Russian Tu-214PU aircraft took off from Moscow and landed in Tehran at around 10:10 am on July 13. The deployment coincided with escalating US and Israeli airstrikes on Iranian military infrastructure
item.content:encoded: A Russian Tu-214PU aircraft took off from Moscow and landed in Tehran at around 10:10 am on July 13. The deployment coincided with escalating US and Israeli airstrikes on Iranian military infrastructure

item.title: How A German App Is Turning Shade Into A Lifeline During Heatwaves

media:content.media:description: Adapting to climate change is no longer a future concern but a public health challenge.

item.description: As Europe reels from extreme heatwaves, a German app is helping people stay cool by mapping shaded walking routes. The app uses sun and shadow data to guide commuters away from heat-exposed streets.
item.content:encoded: As Europe reels from extreme heatwaves, a German app is helping people stay cool by mapping shaded walking routes. The app uses sun and shadow data to guide commuters away from heat-exposed streets.

item.title: Top AI Models Are Less Critical Of Repressive Regimes: Meta Report

media:content.media:description: AI companies urged to conduct systematic human rights analyses. (Representational)

item.description: A study showed AI services were echoing the rules of countries that restrict speech and that bias could creep into services used by an increasing number of users.
item.content:encoded: A study showed AI services were echoing the rules of countries that restrict speech and that bias could creep into services used by an increasing number of users.

item.title: 'Cowardly Crime Against Children': Iran Slams US Attack Near Cancer Hospital

media:content.media:description: The hospital which provided chemotherapy treatment for cancer patients had to be evacuated

item.description: Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei said that the attack was "reminiscent of Israel's atrocities against healthcare facilities". He said that the attack caused severe suffering and anxiety to the children who were hospitalised.
item.content:encoded: Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei said that the attack was "reminiscent of Israel's atrocities against healthcare facilities". He said that the attack caused severe suffering and anxiety to the children who were hospitalised.

item.title: New Zealand Issues Tsunami Warning After 6.3-Magnitude Earthquake
item.description: The epicenter of the earthquake was about 40 km north of Te Anau, which is the gateway to the tourist hotspot of Fiordland.
item.content:encoded: The epicenter of the earthquake was about 40 km north of Te Anau, which is the gateway to the tourist hotspot of Fiordland.

item.title: US Mint Begins Production Of Controversial $1 Coin Bearing Trump's Face

media:content.media:description: The front of the coin features an image of Trump in a suit and tie and with a stern look on his face.

item.description: The coin is intended "to honor the enduring legacy of liberty and a lasting symbol of patriotism," Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.
item.content:encoded: The coin is intended "to honor the enduring legacy of liberty and a lasting symbol of patriotism," Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.

item.title: US Considers Permanent Daylight Saving Time Again. How It Could Work

media:content.media:description: Currently, the shift is forward in spring and back to standard time in fall. (Representational)

item.description: The House of Representatives on Tuesday voted to pass a bill to shift daylight saving time, when clocks are moved forward one hour, become permanent.
item.content:encoded: The House of Representatives on Tuesday voted to pass a bill to shift daylight saving time, when clocks are moved forward one hour, become permanent.

item.title: "We Will Kill Trump": Iran's New Billboard Puts US President In A Coffin

media:content.media:description: Iran has long used public spaces as a tool of political communication.

item.description: The new hoarding shows the US president's body peeking out from an open black coffin. In the picture, his hair is disheveled, and his eyes and mouth are closed, as his hands rest on a red tie atop a protruding stomach.
item.content:encoded: The new hoarding shows the US president's body peeking out from an open black coffin. In the picture, his hair is disheveled, and his eyes and mouth are closed, as his hands rest on a red tie atop a protruding stomach.

item.title: Opinion: Opinion | Why Britain Is Finding It So Hard To Deport A Pakistani 'Grooming Gang' Leader
item.description: Shabir Ahmed, a UK-Pakistan dual national, lost his British citizenship after his conviction more than two decades ago. Now, Pakistan is refusing to take him back.
item.content:encoded: Shabir Ahmed, a UK-Pakistan dual national, lost his British citizenship after his conviction more than two decades ago. Now, Pakistan is refusing to take him back.

item.title: Gangster Nitish Kaushal, 26, Added To FBI's Wanted List

media:content.media:description: Nitish Kaushal is part of the Bhagwanpuria Organized Crime Group.

item.description: Gangster Nitish Kaushal, alias Lala, is accused of carrying out act of violence, including, but not limited to kidnappings and assaults.
item.content:encoded: Gangster Nitish Kaushal, alias Lala, is accused of carrying out act of violence, including, but not limited to kidnappings and assaults.

item.title: "Allows To Operate At Absolute Best": US To Test Troops For Low Testosterone

media:content.media:description: Pete Hegseth said the screening program will test "testosterone deficiency" among troops (File photo)

item.description: The screenings will be conducted annually as part of service members' required medical screenings for those 30 and older.
item.content:encoded: The screenings will be conducted annually as part of service members' required medical screenings for those 30 and older.

item.title: Former Cargo Ship Captain Faces Prison For Drugging, Raping US Marine Cadet

media:content.media:description: He admitted that he had sex with the cadet without her consent on the ship (Representational)

item.description: John Merrone, 53, pleaded guilty just as his trial was about to start in Brooklyn federal court.
item.content:encoded: John Merrone, 53, pleaded guilty just as his trial was about to start in Brooklyn federal court.

item.title: 'Strait Of Hormuz Is A Red Line': Iran's Big Warning To Trump Over US Strikes

media:content.media:description: Hormuz carried about a fifth of global oil and gas shipments before war (Representational Image)

item.description: The US launched a fifth night of attacks on Wednesday and reimposed a naval blockade of Iran's ports.
item.content:encoded: The US launched a fifth night of attacks on Wednesday and reimposed a naval blockade of Iran's ports.

item.title: Qatar Jet Leak Probe Intensifies, US Officials Asked To Hand Over Phones

media:content.media:description: White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles and FBI Director Kash Patel led the investigation

item.description: The investigation became public after The New York Times reported that the US Justice Department had issued subpoenas to four of its journalists who wrote about the plane's security issues.
item.content:encoded: The investigation became public after The New York Times reported that the US Justice Department had issued subpoenas to four of its journalists who wrote about the plane's security issues.

item.title: India Reacts To Stabbing Of Hyderabad Man For His Religion In Utah

media:content.media:description: The Utah stabbing has renewed concerns over the safety of Indians in the United States.

item.description: The man that stabbed Syed Sohailuddin told police he did so because "he believes he is a catalyst and he intends to kill Muslims."
item.content:encoded: The man that stabbed Syed Sohailuddin told police he did so because "he believes he is a catalyst and he intends to kill Muslims."

item.title: France Approves Historic Assisted-Dying Bill After Years Of Debate

media:content.media:description: Patients seeking to end their lives would have to be at least 18 years old (Representational)

item.description: France, a traditionally Catholic nation, has grappled with legal, medical, moral and religious questions about end-of-life options.
item.content:encoded: France, a traditionally Catholic nation, has grappled with legal, medical, moral and religious questions about end-of-life options.

item.title: Over 500 Feared Dead, Mostly Rohingyas, After 2 Boats Sink Off Myanmar Coast

media:content.media:description: The Rohingya exodus from Rakhine started in 2017 (File)

item.description: The two vessels left Myanmar's Rakhine State in late June carrying mostly ethnic minority Rohingya passengers, reportedly including some from refugee camps in Bangladesh.
item.content:encoded: The two vessels left Myanmar's Rakhine State in late June carrying mostly ethnic minority Rohingya passengers, reportedly including some from refugee camps in Bangladesh.

item.title: India's Big Move To Protect Indian Sailors Amid US-Iran Conflict In Hormuz

media:content.media:description: India is the world's third-largest supplier of seafarers, with more than 300,000 sailors

item.description: The advisory comes amid heightened tensions in the Middle East after US strikes on Iranian military installations stoked fears of a wider regional conflict and potential disruptions to shipping through Hormuz.
item.content:encoded: The advisory comes amid heightened tensions in the Middle East after US strikes on Iranian military installations stoked fears of a wider regional conflict and potential disruptions to shipping through Hormuz.

item.title: Indian Teen's Father Seeks Ban on Horse Carriages After Deadly New York Accident

media:content.media:description: Indian teen Deepak Mahajan killed in horse carriage accident.

item.description: This was not an unpredictable accident, it was a safety violation and the system's failure to protect the people who visit the city, says the father.
item.content:encoded: This was not an unpredictable accident, it was a safety violation and the system's failure to protect the people who visit the city, says the father.

item.title: Opinion: Opinion | Why A Post By A Senior US Defence Official About 'Allies' Should Worry India
item.description: Colby's post frames what could become a thorny question in India-US relations.
item.content:encoded: Colby's post frames what could become a thorny question in India-US relations.
